response:
Short answer: because APIM and our existing platform tooling expect and validate Rackspace identity tokens, and reusing the already‑implemented Rackspace Identity flow was simpler and more reliable than introducing Entra client credentials.

Why in more detail:
- APIM’s X-Auth-Token flow is implemented to validate Rackspace identity tokens, classify callers (racker vs customer), map group/role named‑values and inject trusted headers the backend relies on. Using a Rackspace token ensures APIM will accept the call and forward the expected headers.
- We already had a working Rackspace Identity path in the codebase (shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and RACKSPACE_USERNAME/RACKSPACE_PASSWORD env vars), so reusing it avoided new secret provisioning and extra Entra app/audience configuration.
- Using Entra client credentials introduced extra deployment and JWT‑validation complexity (audiences, tenant config) and had proven brittle in prod (missing tenant/audience config caused auth failures). Reusing the existing Rackspace flow is less error‑prone in our deployed environments.
context_injected:
{"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "debug", "facts": ["Rackspace token → manages → APIM", "Rackspace token → integrates_with → agent-fleet-api", "Rackspace token → used_by → ApiManagementGatewayLogs", "Rackspace token → targets → APIM authentication debugging pattern", "Rackspace token → uses → APIM on-error policy", "Rackspace token → implements → APIM rewrite logic (/v1 preservation)", "Rackspace token → configured_with → api/openapi-apim.yaml", "Rackspace token → integrates_with → App Insights", "Rackspace token → targets → Application Gateway", "Rackspace token → contained_in → azr-fleet-platform-infra", "Rackspace token → integrates_with → azr-fleet-platform-prod", "Rackspace token → depends_on → Canonical PolicyEngine.Read/Write/Admin roles", "Rackspace token → common_failure_point_for → DDI tenant isolation", "Rackspace token → targets → Event Grid", "X-Auth-Token → uses → api_audit_all"], "recent": ["2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Fleet Event Grid remediation token acquisition was revised again on 2026-04-09: instead of Entra client credentials, backend-py now reuses the existing Rackspace Identity flow backed by RACKSPACE_USERNAME and RACKSPACE_PASSWORD. shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that token as Authorization: Bearer to APIM for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3; component: backend-py switched auth from Entra client credentials to Rackspace Identity flow; env_vars: RACKSPACE_USERNAME, RACKSPACE_PASSWORD; caller_file: shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py; called_function: shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity'); auth_mode: identity; auth_header: Authorization: Bearer; target_endpoint: APIM Event Grid ensure endpoint]", "2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscript
[specifics: environment: azr-compliance-v3; sync_component: SyncEventGrid; scoping_rule: join customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations requiring managed_by_tenant_id = Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in [active,succeeded]; credential_usage: single Rackspace Lighthouse credential (no per-customer tenant tokens); event_subscription_name_pattern: rax-/rackspace-; destination_patterns: rackspace.com; compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts; known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs; GetEventGridDeployments_scope: summary and list use same managed monitoring scope (only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions); sync_stats_categories: skipped_unmanaged_subscriptions; stale_or_missing (SubscriptionNotFound warnings); filtered_unmanaged_deployments]", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation; credential_type: ClientSecretCredential; token_scope: api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default; apim_subscription_endpoint: https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription; frontend_request: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure; reason_for_backend_flow: Fleet requires application permission; backend_behavior: polls returned statusUrl until completion; previous_flow: browser-side Fleet API call]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.
[specifics: app: azr-compliance-v3; remediation_call: browser-side Fleet Platform call (instead of backend proxy); auth_reason: browser has operator MSAL Bearer token; backend behind APIM may not retain raw Authorization credential; metadata_field: metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi); env_var_scope: VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE (optional Fleet-specific scope requested); api_call: POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL; polling_mechanism: polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus; success_conditions: treats succeeded and already_exists as success; error_mappings: 404 -> not_found; 409 -> not_eligible; ui_changes: per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges]", "2026-04-02: On 2026-04-02 in /Users/benj8956/Documents/RackspaceClaw, added the first actual Entra-aware proxy implementation assets for the RackspaceClaw POC. Added runtime-config/proxy/docker-compose.proxy.yml, oauth2-proxy.cfg.template, default.conf.template, proxy.env.example, TLS cert placeholder docs, and docs/entra-proxy-poc.md. The proxy stack uses nginx in front of oauth2-proxy with provider=entra-id and passes X-Auth-Request-Email to the upstream, matching OpenClaw trusted-proxy guidance. Also updated scripts/serve_poc.py so the upstream honors trusted-proxy identity headers and exposes proxy context for debugging. Verified locally by sending X-Auth-Request-Email and related forwarded headers to /api/me and /api/proxy-context; the app reported auth_source=trusted-proxy and returned the forwarded values correctly.
[specifics: repo_path: /Users/benj8956/Documents/RackspaceClaw; added_files: runtime-config/proxy/docker-compose.proxy.yml, oauth2-proxy.cfg.template, default.conf.template, proxy.env.example, docs/entra-proxy-poc.md, TLS cert placeholder docs; script_updated: scripts/serve_poc.py (now honors trusted-proxy identity headers and exposes proxy context for debugging); architecture: nginx in front of oauth2-proxy; oauth2_provider: provider=entra-id; forwarded_header: X-Auth-Request-Email; tested_endpoints: /api/me, /api/proxy-context (verified locally by sending X-Auth-Request-Email and related forwarded headers); app_reported: auth_source=trusted-proxy]", "2026-03-23: Rackspace Identity token mint responses do not return x-tenant-id/DDI for operator/Racker accounts. The identity response is only a token mint/lookup payload; x-tenant-id must be supplied from platform context such as customer_subscriptions.ddi or another authoritative customer mapping, not inferred from the minted token response. For Fleet Platform operator API calls that use x-auth-token, do not expect the mint response to provide tenant context.
[specifics: token_mint_response: no_x-tenant-id_or_DDI_for_operator_or_Racker_accounts; x-tenant-id_source: must_be_supplied_from_platform_context_like_customer_subscriptions.ddi_or_other_authoritative_customer_mapping; tenant_inference_warning: do_not_infer_tenant_context_from_minted_token_response; fleet_platform_operator_api_x-auth-token: mint_response_will_not_provide_tenant_context_for_operator_calls]", "2026-03-19: In azr-compliance-v3, X-Auth-Token authorization is defined in APIM, not OpenAPI. APIM validates the Rackspace token, classifies the caller as racker or customer, maps racker named-value groups to MSP-Admin/MSP-Engineer/MSP-Viewer, maps customer roles to admin/billing/observer, enforces route/category RBAC in api-policy.xml, then forwards trusted headers like X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, and X-DDI to the backend. The actual racker group display-name values are environment-configured APIM named values, not stored in repo.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3; auth_config_location: APIM (X-Auth-Token) not OpenAPI; apim_validates: Rackspace token; caller_classification: racker or customer; racker_named_value_groups: MSP-Admin, MSP-Engineer, MSP-Viewer; customer_role_mappings: admin, billing, observer; rbac_policy_file: api-policy.xml (enforces route/category RBAC); forwarded_headers: X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, X-DDI; racker_group_display_names: environment-configured APIM named values (not stored in repo)]", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, targeted Event Grid remediation API failures in prod had two independent causes. First, azr-fleet-platform-prod was missing AUTH_TENANT_ID and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES, causing backend function auth to return 'JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing' and 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted. Fix: set AUTH_TENANT_ID=570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES=api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f,3eabce94-dd29-4358-b262-09a43f2ddb60 on azr-fleet-platform-prod and update deploy workflows to keep them. Second, the public APIM route still failed even after auth was fixed because agent-fleet-api APIM policy validated only old audience api://agent-fleet-platform and rewrote targeted Event Grid management paths to backend /api/customers/... instead of /api/v1/customers/.... ApiManagementGatewayLogs confirmed 404 backend URL https://azr-fleet-platform-prod.../api/customers/{ddi}/subscriptions/{sub}/eventgrid/subscription for correlation ids dc96056e-e73b-4775-9f1d-7e9c54d0c2cc and 6cd9e891-3f89-4fa1-b3cc-9841e3f39349. Repo fix is to add {{api-audience}} as
[specifics: resource: azr-fleet-platform-prod; AUTH_TENANT_ID: 570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b; AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES: api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f,3eabce94-dd29-4358-b262-09a43f2ddb60; backend_error_message: JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing; backend_http_status: 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted; old_apim_audience: api://agent-fleet-platform; apim_rewrite_bug: rewritten backend path /api/v1/customers/.../.../eventgrid/... -> /api/customers/.../.../eventgrid/ (missing /v1); backend_404_url_pattern: https://azr-fleet-platform-prod.../api/customers/{ddi}/subscriptions/{sub}/eventgrid/subscription; correlation_ids: dc96056e-e73b-4775-9f1d-7e9c54d0c2cc, 6cd9e891-3f89-4fa1-b3cc-9841e3f39349; repo_fix: add {{api-audience}} as JWT audience in global-policy*.xml and preserve /v1 in APIM rewrite logic; maintain api-audience named value in deploy workflows]"]}
